content.full_text:
February 15, 1977

Honorable James O. Miller  
Municipal Court of San Mateo County  
South San Francisco, CA 94080

Re: Opinion Request No. 77-001

Dear Judge Miller:

Thank you for your letter of January 24, 1977, requesting an opinion regarding whether the payments for your expenses by the San Mateo Elks Lodge No. 812 are reportable on your Statement of Economic Interests. Because your request does not involve a substantial question of interpretation of the Act, no formal opinion will be issued. However, I hope the following informal comments will be helpful.

Government Code Section 82030(a) provides that income includes "income of any nature from any source, including ... reimbursement for expenses." On the other hand Government Code Section 82030(b) establishes certain exceptions to the general definition of income, including "reimbursement for travel expenses and per diem received from a bona fide educational, academic or charitable organization." Government Code Section 82030(b)(2). Pursuant to these provisions, the payments you receive from the Elks Lodge will be reportable as income unless the Elks Lodge is a bona fide educational, academic or charitable organization.

We have concluded that the Elks Lodge is not such an organization. The Elks Lodge is exempt from federal taxes by reason of its status as a fraternal beneficiary society, order or association. Section 501(c)(8) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1954. We think that this appellation accurately reflects the character of the Elks Lodge and that it is not a bona fide educational, academic or charitable organization within the meaning of Government Code Section 82030(b)(2). Accordingly, payments from that organization for your expenses are income and will be reportable if they total $250 or more.

Honorable James O. Miller  
Page Two  
February 15, 1977

If you wish to appeal the denial of your opinion request you may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18321. If you have any further questions please contact Kenneth Finney, Chief of the Commission's Legal Division.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Michael Bennett  
Executive Director

MB:KF:jo
